name: Northern Ireland
summary: Northern Ireland operates a distinct common-law family-law jurisdiction within the United Kingdom. The substantive statute is the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (Sewel-style Order in Council mirroring Children Act 1989 but distinct), litigated in the High Court of Justice and Court of Judicature of Northern Ireland. The Lady Chief Justice Rt Hon Dame Siobhan Keegan DBE KC (sworn 2.9.2021, first woman LCJ in NI history) was previously a family-law silk and Senior Family Judge of the High Court NI (2020-2021), giving NI's family judiciary unusual structural sophistication. The clearest current NI judicial engagement with alienating-style dynamic — without explicit PA label — is Mr Justice O'Hara's August 2022 judgment in A Father v A Mother (re NI, male child aged 10) (No. 2). Institutionally the critique pole is Women's Aid Federation NI (WAFNI), whose 25 June 2020 PA briefing is the only NI-specific WA published institutional position. NI Family Bar Association (Bar of NI) is institutional bar gateway.

statutory_framework.title: Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 — substantive children-law statute
statutory_framework.relevance: Substantive NI children-law statute. Sewel-style Order in Council mirroring Children Act 1989 substantive frame but with NI-distinct numbering and procedural provisions. Art 3 welfare paramountcy principle equivalent to s.1 Children Act 1989.

statutory_framework.citation: Family Homes and Domestic Violence (Northern Ireland) Order 1998
statutory_framework.title: Family Homes and Domestic Violence (NI) Order 1998
statutory_framework.relevance: NI-distinct DV-protection statute. Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021 subsequently introduced coercive-control offence parallel to E&W Serious Crime Act 2015 s.76.

statutory_framework.citation: Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (Northern Ireland) 2021
statutory_framework.title: Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021 — coercive control criminalisation
statutory_framework.relevance: NI-specific coercive-control statute introduced via Assembly legislation (effective 21.2.2022). Operates parallel to E&W and Scottish coercive-control regimes. Re-frames evidential backdrop for PA-adjacent NI fact-patterns.

statutory_framework.title: Judicature (NI) Act 1978 — constitutes NI court structure
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutes the Court of Judicature of Northern Ireland (Court of Appeal NI + High Court NI). Family Division of the High Court NI is the substantive forum for higher-value family-law disputes.

key_decisions.caption: UKSC is the final court of appeal in NI civil matters. No NI-specific PA-construct apex decision has reached the UKSC.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Northern Ireland

key_decisions.caption: Court of Appeal NI — appellate jurisdiction over NI family-law decisions. Lady Chief Justice Rt Hon Dame Siobhan Keegan DBE KC presides. No express-PA CofA NI decision identified at the level of England's Re-arc.

apex_courts.name: High Court of Justice in Northern Ireland — Family Division

key_decisions.caption: A Father v A Mother (re NI, male child aged 10 years) (No. 2) — Mr Justice O'Hara, August 2022. Found one parent's 'core belief that he had been wronged' was incompatible with facilitating contact while declining to transfer residence. Clearest current NI judicial engagement with alienating-style dynamic without explicit PA label.
First-instance lay magistrate forum for NI family-law disputes under Children (NI) Order 1995. The bulk of NI PA-adjacent fact-patterns are decided at this level.

professional_regulators.name: Bar of Northern Ireland — Family Bar Association
professional_regulators.position_statement: NI Family Bar Association is the institutional gateway to the practising NI family bar. No formal NI-specific PA position issued; engagement at individual-practitioner level.

professional_regulators.name: Law Society of Northern Ireland
professional_regulators.position_statement: Statutory regulator for NI solicitors. No PA-specific NI position.

professional_regulators.name: Northern Ireland Guardian Ad Litem Agency (NIGALA)
professional_regulators.position_statement: NI's statutory child-welfare reporting body — NI analogue to England's Cafcass and Wales' Cafcass Cymru. Provides guardian ad litem service in family proceedings. No NI-specific PA position; engagement at individual-case welfare-report level.

professional_regulators.name: Women's Aid Federation Northern Ireland (WAFNI)
professional_regulators.position_statement: NI federation-aligned women's-aid body. 25 June 2020 PA briefing is the ONLY NI-specific WA published institutional position on the parental-alienation construct. Critique-camp institutional anchor for NI.

professional_regulators.name: Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC)
professional_regulators.position_statement: UK-wide statutory regulator for practitioner psychologists. Applies in NI. No NI-specific PA position.

professional_regulators.name: British Psychological Society — Northern Ireland branch
professional_regulators.position_statement: BPS NI branch — operational subsidiary of UK-wide BPS. No NI-specific PA position.

professional_regulators.name: Queen's University Belfast (QUB) — Dr Mairead McCormack DChild 2021
professional_regulators.position_statement: QUB-based DChild 2021 thesis is the only NI-specific empirical study of how PA is treated by NI family-court professionals. Single NI academic anchor in PA-specific field.

anonymisation_convention: NI family-court judgments anonymise children by initial; adult parties may be named or anonymised depending on case sensitivity. Judiciaryni.uk publishes consolidated NI judgments. Children Act 1989-equivalent reporting restrictions under Children (NI) Order 1995 apply.

key_developments.description: Government of Ireland Act 1920 effective 3 May 1921 + Anglo-Irish Treaty 6 December 1921 — substantively distinctive globally Northern-Ireland-as-distinct-UK-jurisdiction-+-Irish-Free-State-+-partition framework. Foundational substantive 105-year Northern-Ireland-within-UK framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.
Judicature (NI) Act 1978 — constitutes NI court structure.
Children (NI) Order 1995 — substantive NI children-law statute (Sewel-style Order in Council).

Good Friday Agreement 10 April 1998 + Northern Ireland Act 1998 — substantively distinctive globally Belfast-Agreement-+-power-sharing-Stormont-Assembly + cross-border-North-South-Ministerial-Council framework. Family Homes and Domestic Violence (NI) Order 1998 — NI-distinct DV-protection statute.

8 May 2007 — Stormont Assembly + Executive restored after 2002-2007 suspension under St Andrews Agreement 13 October 2006 — substantively distinctive UK NI-Executive-Stormont-Assembly-restored framework. Substantive devolution framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.
April 2020 - September 2021 — Mrs Justice Keegan as Senior Family Judge of the High Court NI. 25 June 2020 — WAFNI PA briefing (only NI-specific WA institutional position).
2 September 2021 — Rt Hon Dame Siobhan Keegan DBE KC sworn as Lady Chief Justice of Northern Ireland (first woman in NI history). McCormack DChild thesis (QUB) — first NI-specific empirical PA study.

21 February 2022 — Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021 coercive-control offence in force. August 2022 — Mr Justice O'Hara A Father v A Mother (No. 2) — clearest current NI judicial engagement with alienating-style dynamic.

Windsor Framework 27 February 2023 substantively reshaping post-Brexit Northern-Ireland-Protocol — substantively distinctive globally only-UK-jurisdiction-with-bespoke-EU-acquis-applicability-+-Northern-Ireland-Protocol-+-Stormont-Brake framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent family-law-implementation including continuing EU-Brussels-IIa-applicability.

Stormont Assembly + Executive restored 3 February 2024 ending 2-year DUP boycott + Michelle O'Neill Sinn Féin First Minister + Emma Little-Pengelly DUP deputy First Minister — substantively distinctive globally first-Sinn-Féin-First-Minister-of-Northern-Ireland framework. Continuing NIGALA + WAFNI + Bar of NI institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Children (NI) Order 1995 + DA&CP NI Act 2021 framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

2026 — Court of Appeal of Northern Ireland + High Court of Justice in Northern Ireland Family Division + Lady Chief Justice Keegan continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children (NI) Order 1995 + Family Homes and Domestic Violence (NI) Order 1998 + Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021 + Windsor Framework 2023 + UK-wide Re Y EWFC 38 framework in custody disputes within continuing Stormont Assembly + post-Brexit Northern-Ireland-Protocol context. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: LADY CHIEF JUSTICE KEEGAN AS LOAD-BEARING JUDICIAL FIGURE: Rt Hon Dame Siobhan Keegan DBE KC, sworn 2.9.2021, is the FIRST WOMAN LADY CHIEF JUSTICE IN NI HISTORY and the only Lady Chief Justice in the UK whose pre-judicial career was as a family-law silk. Senior Family Judge of the High Court NI 2020-2021 → LCJ. Gives NI's family judiciary unusual structural sophistication on child-welfare issues.

O'HARA J A FATHER v A MOTHER (No. 2) AUGUST 2022 IS THE CLEAREST CURRENT NI JUDICIAL ENGAGEMENT with alienating-style dynamic — without explicit PA label. Found 'core belief that he had been wronged' incompatible with facilitating contact while declining to transfer residence. Functional analogue to recognition-camp reasoning expressed in welfare/abuse vocabulary.

DISTINCT STATUTORY ARCHITECTURE: Children (NI) Order 1995 + Family Homes and DV (NI) Order 1998 + Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021. NI operates a Sewel-style Order in Council frame distinct from Children Act 1989 E&W statutory frame.

NIGALA AS NI INSTITUTIONAL ANALOGUE: Northern Ireland Guardian Ad Litem Agency is the statutory child-welfare reporting body — NI analogue to English Cafcass + Welsh Cafcass Cymru. Operational PA-adjacent engagement at welfare-report level.
WAFNI 25 JUNE 2020 PA BRIEFING IS THE ONLY NI-SPECIFIC WA INSTITUTIONAL POSITION: Women's Aid Federation NI is the load-bearing NI critique-camp institutional anchor. Single document anchor.

MCCORMACK QUB DCHILD 2021 IS ONLY NI ACADEMIC ANCHOR IN PA-SPECIFIC FIELD: Dr Mairead McCormack's QUB DChild 2021 thesis is the only NI-specific empirical study of PA treatment by NI family-court professionals. NI academic footprint outside the judiciary thin.

PRESERVED CORRECTIONS FROM UK-DEVOLVED LAWYERS V2: 'Mary Connolly KC' does not exist on Bar of NI roll (confused with Martina Connolly KC); Jonathan Dunlop KC is commercial/JR/professional-negligence specialist, not family. NI bar verification baseline established.

sources.title: NIGALA — Northern Ireland Guardian Ad Litem Agency

editorial_notes: Northern Ireland jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 6 to 10 key_developments with full Government-of-Ireland-Act-1921-to-2026 trajectory: 1921-Government-of-Ireland-Act-+-partition + 1978-Judicature-NI-Act + 1995-Children-NI-Order + 1998-Good-Friday-Agreement-+-Family-Homes-and-DV-NI-Order + 2007-Stormont-Assembly-restored + 2020-Mrs-Justice-Keegan-Senior-Family-Judge-+-WAFNI-PA-briefing + 2021-Dame-Siobhan-Keegan-LCJ-+-McCormack-DChild + 2022-DA&CP-NI-Act-+-O'Hara-A-Father-v-A-Mother + 2023-Windsor-Framework + 2026-Court-of-Appeal-NI-+-Stormont-Assembly-+-post-Brexit-NI-Protocol.
Primary-source order: judiciaryni.uk for case-law; legislation.gov.uk for NI statutes; Bar of NI + Law Society NI for regulatory; QUB for academic anchor.
NI treated as distinct id with unique statutory architecture (Children NI Order 1995 + 1998 FH&DV Order + 2021 DA&CP Act) but appellate to UKSC.
Lady Chief Justice Keegan judicial-sophistication preserved in structural_findings[0] as load-bearing NI feature.
O'Hara J A Father v A Mother (No. 2) August 2022 preserved as clearest NI judicial PA-adjacent engagement.
Preserved corrections from UK-Devolved v2 lawyers file (Mary Connolly KC does not exist; Dunlop KC commercial not family) in structural_findings[6].

Joins UK-NI-devolved + common-law + Government-of-Ireland-Act-1921-+-partition + Good-Friday-Agreement-1998-+-Stormont-Assembly-+-power-sharing + post-Brexit-Northern-Ireland-Protocol-+-Windsor-Framework-2023-only-UK-jurisdiction-with-bespoke-EU-acquis-applicability-globally-distinctive + Children-NI-Order-1995-Sewel-style-Order-in-Council + DA&CP-NI-Act-2021-coercive-control + Lady-Chief-Justice-Keegan-first-woman-LCJ-+-family-law-silk-+-Senior-Family-Judge + NIGALA-NI-institutional-analogue-to-Cafcass + O'Hara-J-A-Father-v-A-Mother-No-2-August-2022 + WAFNI-25-June-2020-PA-briefing-only-NI-specific-WA-institutional-position + Cuthbert-QUB-DChild-2021 clusters within the corpus.
